 {"id":"bd-1764","title":"Query Intelligence: Understanding and Improving Queries","description":"# Query Intelligence

## The Problem
Agents often struggle with queries:
1. **Why did this return 0 results?** - Was the query malformed? Too specific?
2. **How was my query interpreted?** - Did it understand my intent?
3. **What would work better?** - Suggestions for improvement

## The Solution
Make the query engine transparent and helpful:
- Explain how queries are parsed and executed
- Suggest alternatives when queries fail
- Allow dry-run validation

## Subtasks
1. **rob.query.explain** - Query explanation (--explain)
2. **rob.query.suggest** - Suggestions and did-you-mean
3. **rob.query.dry** - Dry-run mode (--dry-run)

## Value for Agents
- Self-correction: Agents can fix their own queries
- Learning: Understand query syntax through examples
- Confidence: Know when a query is reliable","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-30T23:54:08.420280752Z","updated_at":"2025-12-15T06:23:15.004523524Z","closed_at":"2025-12-02T05:04:11.376139Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-1942","title":"A1.1: Indexing status banner in results area","description":"Display a prominent banner in the results area when indexing is in progress, with progress bar and estimated completion.","notes":"**Implementation**:
1. In tui.rs render loop (around line 1706-1742), when panes.is_empty() AND progress.phase != 0:
   - Show dedicated indexing banner instead of 'No results'
   - Include spinner animation (reuse SPINNER_CHARS)
   - Show progress bar using indicatif-style blocks
   - Display phase name ('Scanning sources...' / 'Building index...')
   - Add helpful text: 'Search will be available once indexing completes'

2. When panes are NOT empty but indexing is in progress:
   - Add subtle banner at top of results: '⚠ Indexing in progress - results may be incomplete'
   - Use warning color from palette (amber/yellow)

**Files to modify**:
- src/ui/tui.rs: render logic for results area
- src/ui/components/widgets.rs: optional new widget for progress banner

**Testing**:
- Verify banner appears during cass index --full
- Verify banner clears when indexing completes
- Test with TUI_HEADLESS=1 for snapshot","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-28T22:00:00Z","updated_at":"2025-11-29T06:44:55.546609405Z","closed_at":"2025-11-29T06:44:55.546609405Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Use same cache shards; mark warm entries for metrics.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-26T22:50:12.147739525Z","updated_at":"2025-11-30T06:24:00.654173281Z","closed_at":"2025-11-30T06:24:00.654173281Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["performance","search"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-19ff","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-glt.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:43Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-19ff","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-glt.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:43Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1m6m","title":"[EPIC] HTML Session Export: Beautiful, Encrypted, Cross-Platform","description":"# Epic: Premium HTML Session Export

## Overview
Create a world-class feature for exporting coding agent sessions as beautifully formatted, 
self-contained static HTML files. This feature provides both TUI and robot mode access,
with optional password-based encryption using Web Crypto API for client-side decryption.

## Strategic Goals
1. **Knowledge Preservation**: Allow users to archive and share valuable coding sessions
2. **Cross-Agent Value**: Export sessions from ANY agent (Claude, Codex, Cursor, etc.)
3. **Security-First**: Optional encryption ensures sensitive content stays protected
4. **Universal Access**: Files work offline, on any device, with or without internet
5. **Stripe-Level UX**: Premium visual design matching cass's TUI quality standards

## Key Features
- Self-contained HTML files with embedded CSS/JS
- Progressive enhancement: works offline, enhanced with CDN (Tailwind, Prism.js)
- Optional AES-256-GCM encryption with PBKDF2 key derivation
- Cross-platform download folder detection (Linux, macOS, Windows)
- Smart file naming: agent_project_datetime_topic.html
- Beautiful conversation rendering with syntax highlighting
- Dark/light mode toggle in exported file
- Print-friendly styles for PDF export from browser
- Search within exported conversation
- Collapsible tool calls and code blocks
- Full TUI modal with preview and options
- Complete robot mode API for automation

## Technical Approach
- Rust-side: HTML template generation with minified inline CSS/JS
- Encryption: PBKDF2 (WebCrypto-compatible) + AES-GCM with explicit salt/iv/iterations
- Web Crypto: PBKDF2 for key derivation (client-side compatible)
- File naming: Sanitize and slugify with chrono timestamps
- Downloads: dirs::download_dir() with fallback strategies

## Research Sources
- StatiCrypt: https://github.com/robinmoisson/staticrypt (static HTML encryption patterns)
- Web Crypto API: https://gist.github.com/chrisveness/43bcda93af9f646d083fad678071b90a
- Tailwind Chat UI: https://flowbite.com/docs/components/chat-bubble/
- Prism.js CDN: https://prismjs.com/ (syntax highlighting)
- Print CSS: https://print-css.rocks/ (print-friendly styles)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Export works for all 11 supported agents
- [ ] File opens correctly in Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge
- [ ] Encryption/decryption works in all browsers
- [ ] Offline mode displays conversation correctly
- [ ] Print to PDF produces readable output
- [ ] TUI modal is intuitive and polished
- [ ] Robot mode provides complete API coverage","notes":"### QA Gate (must satisfy before closing)
- Unit tests for renderer, encryption, filename, JS/CSS injection, and CLI arg parsing.
- Integration tests for full export pipeline + log verification.
- E2E browser tests across Chrome/Firefox/Safari (or WebKit) with detailed logs and metrics.
- Publish a consolidated test report (JSON + markdown) with version, data hash, and environment.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-25T20:26:49.360766997Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T05:00:37.131138616Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T05:00:37.131119921Z","close_reason":"All 16 subtasks completed. HTML export feature fully implemented with: core rendering, encryption (AES-256-GCM), CDN fallback, print styles, TUI modal, robot API, test fixtures, logging, unit tests, integration tests, and E2E browser tests (171 passing across Chrome/Firefox/Safari). Acceptance criteria verified through comprehensive test coverage.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["html-export"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1m6m","depends_on_id":"bd-11u5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:45Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1m6m","depends_on_id":"bd-120r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:45Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1m6m","depends_on_id":"bd-1612","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:45Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1m6m","depends_on_id":"bd-1ics","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:45Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1m6m","depends_on_id":"bd-24uo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:45Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1m6m","depends_on_id":"bd-2hfc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:45Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1m6m","depends_on_id":"bd-2ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:45Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1m6m","depends_on_id":"bd-2r58","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:45Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1m6m","depends_on_id":"bd-2vyc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:45Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1m6m","depends_on_id":"bd-5ix1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:45Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1m6m","depends_on_id":"bd-8nnu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:45Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1m6m","depends_on_id":"bd-8w6y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:45Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1m6m","depends_on_id":"bd-bowe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:45Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1m6m","depends_on_id":"bd-bxi5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:45Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1m6m","depends_on_id":"bd-koav","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:45Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1m6m","depends_on_id":"bd-u4me  {"id":"bd-1ueu","title":"Perf: lazy SQLite open to cut startup cost","description":"# Perf: Lazy SQLite Open to Cut Startup Cost

## Goal
Delay opening SQLite until a command actually needs it, reducing CLI/TUI startup time.

## Strategy
- Introduce a lazy initializer for DB pool/connection.
- Keep config parsing and help paths DB-free.
- Ensure thread-safety and single initialization.

## Logging
- Startup timing logs: pre-init, post-init, and reason for DB open.

## Tests
- Unit: lazy init only when DB functions are called; concurrent callers share one open.
- Integration: commands that should not touch DB (help/health) do not open it.
- E2E: startup benchmark script logs cold start times before/after.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Measurable startup reduction (record ms).
- No regressions in DB-required commands.
- Logs include open reason and duration.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T19:20:35.430011738Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T02:34:59.735628215Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T02:34:59.735384155Z","close_reason":"Implemented LazyDb struct in storage/sqlite.rs with parking_lot::Mutex<Option<Connection>>. Updated run_stats, run_context, run_timeline, state_meta_json to use LazyDb. Added lazy_db_to_cli_error helper. 5 unit tests pass. cargo check, clippy, fmt all clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-20af","title":"A6.6: QoL improvements bundle","description":"Collection of smaller quality-of-life improvements for search results display.","notes":"**Improvements**:

1. **Truncation indicators**:
   - Show '...' when content is truncated
   - Different from current ellipsis (use styled indicator)

2. **Word wrap for long lines**:
   - Smart word wrap instead of cut-off
   - Indent continuation lines

3. **Relative timestamps**:
   - '2 hours ago' instead of full timestamp
   - Already partially implemented in format_relative_time()

4. **Result numbering**:
   - Show 1-indexed result numbers for quick reference
   - '#1', '#2', etc. in small text

5. **Match count per result**:
   - Show how many times query matches in this result
   - '(3 matches)' indicator

6. **Agent icon/emoji**:
   - 🤖 Claude | 🔷 Codex | 💎 Gemini | etc.
   - Quick visual identification

7. **Keyboard hint for expansion**:
   - 'Enter for details' hint on selected result

**Files to modify**:
- src/ui/tui.rs: result rendering, helpers
- src/ui/components/theme.rs: if new styles needed

**Testing**:
- Verify each improvement individually
- Test edge cases (very long content, many matches)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-28T22:00:00Z","updated_at":"2025-11-29T06:51:13.188398384Z","closed_at":"2025-11-29T06:51:13.188398384Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-20af","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-sux.6.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:42Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-225c","title":"A7.4: Performance validation","description":"Benchmark and validate that new features don't significantly impact search latency or rendering performance.","notes":"**Benchmarks to run/add**:

1. **Wildcard query latency**:
   - `foo*` vs `foo` (should be similar with edge-ngram)
   - `*foo` (may be slower without reverse index)
   - `*foo*` (may need FTS5 fallback, measure impact)

2. **Fallback overhead**:
   - Time for original + suffix + contains searches
   - Cache hit rates with fallback

3. **Rendering performance**:
   - FPS with 6-8 lines per result vs 2 lines
   - Stripe rendering overhead
   - Border rendering overhead

**Target budgets**:
- Search latency: <80ms (existing target)
- Fallback total: <150ms
- Render FPS: 60fps (existing)

**Files to update**:
- benches/search_perf.rs: add wildcard benchmarks
- benches/runtime_perf.rs: if needed

**If performance issues found**:
- Document in notes
- Consider caching strategies

 {"id":"bd-22k2","title":"P6.14a: Audit remaining mocks/fakes and coverage gaps","description":"# P6.14a: Audit remaining mocks/fakes and coverage gaps

## Goal
Produce an explicit, up-to-date inventory of all mock/fake/stub usage in `src/`, `tests/`, and `scripts/`, plus a gap map for no-mock unit and E2E coverage.

## Why
The repo still contains explicit mock/fake code paths (e.g., MockDaemon in `src/search/daemon_client.rs`, fake binaries in `tests/e2e_install_easy.rs`, fake model file in `tests/semantic_integration.rs`, mock probe helpers in `src/sources/index.rs`). We need a definitive list before removal.

## Tasks
1. `rg -n "Mock|mock|fake|Fake|stub|Stub" -S src tests scripts` and capture all hits.
2. Classify each hit as one of: (a) remove/replace, (b) convert to real fixture, (c) allowlist (true OS/hardware boundary).
3. Map each hit to a downstream task (daemon, sources, install, UI/TUI, semantic model, connectors, CI gating).
4. Identify E2E gaps: flows without script coverage or missing structured logs (e.g., no `scripts/tests/run_all.sh`).

## Deliverable
- `test-results/no_mock_audit.md` with:
  - full hit list + file references
  - classification/decision
  - planned replacement strategy
  - dependencies per subsystem

## Acceptance Criteria
- Audit report exists and is referenced by all follow-up tasks.
- Every mock/fake usage is assigned to a replacement or allowlist path.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-26T06:00:17.295118211Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T06:27:48.197521340Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T06:27:48.197458602Z","close_reason":"Comprehensive audit report created at test-results/no_mock_audit.md.  {"id":"bd-24uo","title":"Implement robot mode API for HTML export automation","description":"# Task: Robot Mode API for HTML Export

## Context
Provide a complete CLI API for automated HTML export, enabling AI agents and
scripts to programmatically export sessions without TUI interaction.

## Command Design

### Primary Command
```bash
cass export-html <session_path> [OPTIONS]
```

### Full Flag Reference
```
cass export-html <session_path>
    --robot                     # JSON output (required for automation)
    --output-dir <path>         # Override output directory (default: downloads)
    --filename <name>           # Override filename (default: auto-generated)
    --encrypt                   # Enable password encryption
    --password <pwd>            # Password (required if --encrypt)
    --password-stdin            # Read password from stdin (secure)
    --include-tools             # Include tool calls in export
    --collapse-threshold <n>    # Collapse messages longer than n chars
    --no-cdns                   # Disable CDN references (fully offline)
    --theme <dark|light>        # Default theme
    --dry-run                   # Validate without writing file
    --explain                   # Show export plan without executing
```

### Batch Export
```bash
cass export-html-batch [OPTIONS]
    --sessions <file>           # File with session paths (one per line)
    --sessions-from -           # Read from stdin
    --query <search>            # Export sessions matching query
    --limit <n>                 # Max sessions to export
    --robot                     # JSON output
    --output-dir <path>         # Output directory for all files
    --encrypt                   # Encrypt all exports
    --password <pwd>            # Shared password for all
```

## JSON Output Schema

### Success Response
```json
{
    "success": true,
    "exported": {
        "session_path": "/path/to/session.jsonl",
        "output_path": "/home/user/Downloads/claude_myproject_2024_01_15_fix_auth.html",
        "filename": "claude_myproject_2024_01_15_fix_auth.html",
        "size_bytes": 87654,
        "encrypted": true,
        "messages_count": 42,
        "agent": "claude_code",
        "workspace": "/projects/myapp",
        "title": "Fix authentication bug"
    },
    "_meta": {
        "elapsed_ms": 234,
        "cass_version": "0.5.0"
    }
}
```

### Batch Success Response
```json
{
    "success": true,
    "exported": [
        { /* session 1 */ },
        { /* session 2 */ }
    ],
    "failed": [
        {
            "session_path": "/path/to/broken.jsonl",
            "error": "Session not found"
        }
    ],
    "summary": {
        "total": 5,
        "exported": 4,
        "failed": 1,
        "total_size_bytes": 456789
    },
    "_meta": {
        "elapsed_ms": 1234
    }
}
```

### Error Response
```json
{
    "success": false,
    "error": {
        "code": 3,
        "kind": "session_not_found",
        "message": "Session file does not exist",
        "hint": "Verify the path or use 'cass search' to find sessions",
        "retryable": false
    }
}
```

## Exit Codes
| Code | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| 0 | Success |
| 1 | General failure |
| 2 | Usage/argument error |
| 3 | Session not found |
| 4 | Output directory not writable |
| 5 | Encryption error |
| 6 | Password required but not provided |

## Implementation

### CLI Parser (extend Commands enum in lib.rs)
```rust
#[derive(Subcommand)]
enum Commands {
    // ... existing commands ...
    
    /// Export session as beautiful HTML file
    #[command(name = "export-html")]
    ExportHtml {
        /// Path to session file
        session: PathBuf,
        
        /// Output directory (default: downloads folder)
        #[arg(long)]
        output_dir: Option<PathBuf>,
        
        /// Custom filename (default: auto-generated)
        #[arg(long)]
        filename: Option<String>,
        
        /// Enable password encryption
        #[arg(long)]
        encrypt: bool,
        
        /// Password for encryption
        #[arg(long)]
        password: Option<String>,
        
        /// Read password from stdin
        #[arg(long)]
        password_stdin: bool,
        
        /// Include tool calls in export
        #[arg(long, default_value_t = true)]
        include_tools: bool,
        
        /// Collapse messages longer than N characters
        #[arg(long)]
        collapse_threshold: Option<usize>,
        
        /// Disable CDN references
        #[arg(long)]
        no_cdns: bool,
        
        /// Default theme
        #[arg(long, default_value = "dark")]
        theme: String,
        
        /// Validate without writing
        #[arg(long)]
        dry_run: bool,
        
        /// Show export plan
        #[arg(long)]
        explain: bool,
    },
    
    /// Batch export multiple sessions
    #[command(name = "export-html-batch")]
    ExportHtmlBatch {
        /// File with session paths
        #[arg(long)]
        sessions: Option<PathBuf>,
        
        /// Read session paths from stdin
        #[arg(long)]
        sessions_from_stdin: bool,
        
        /// Export sessions matching query
        #[arg(long)]
        query: Option<String>,
        
        /// Max sessions to export
        #[arg(long)]
        limit: Option<usize>,
        
        // ... shared options ...
    },
}
```

### Password Handling
```rust
fn get_password(
    password_arg: Option<&str>,
    password_stdin: bool,
    encrypt: bool,
) -> Result<Option<String>, CliError> {
    if !encrypt {
        return Ok(None);
    }
    
    // Priority: explicit arg > stdin > error
    if let Some(pwd) = password_arg {
        return Ok(Some(pwd.to_string()));
    }
    
    if password_stdin {
        let mut pwd = String::new();
        std::io::stdin().read_line(&mut pwd)?;
        return Ok(Some(pwd.trim().to_string()));
    }
    
    Err(CliError::new(6, "password_required", 
        "Password required for encryption", 
        Some("Use --password or --password-stdin")))
}
```

### Explain Mode Output
```json
{
    "plan": {
        "session_path": "/path/to/session.jsonl",
        "agent": "claude_code",
        "messages": 42,
        "output_path": "/home/user/Downloads/claude_myproject_2024_01_15_fix_auth.html",
        "estimated_size_bytes": 87000,
        "options": {
            "encrypted": true,
            "include_tools": true,
            "collapse_threshold": null,
            "cdns_enabled": true,
            "default_theme": "dark"
        }
    },
    "warnings": [
        "Session contains 3 very long messages (>10KB)"
    ]
}
```

## Integration Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_export_html_basic() {
    let result = run_cli(&[
        "export-html", 
        "fixtures/sample_session.jsonl",
        "--robot"
    ]);
    assert!(result.success);
    assert!(result.exported.output_path.exists());
}

#[test]
fn test_export_html_encrypted() {
    let result = run_cli(&[
        "export-html",
        "fixtures/sample_session.jsonl",
        "--encrypt",
        "--password", "test123",
        "--robot"
    ]);
    assert!(result.exported.encrypted);
}

#[test]
fn test_export_html_batch() {
    let result = run_cli(&[
        "export-html-batch",
        "--query", "authentication",
        "--limit", "5",
        "--robot"
    ]);
    assert!(result.summary.total <= 5);
}
```

## Documentation
Add to `cass robot-docs` system:
- `cass robot-docs export-html` - Full API reference
- Examples in robot-docs examples topic

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Single session export works with all flags
- [ ] Batch export handles multiple sessions
- [ ] Password can be provided via arg or stdin
- [ ] JSON output is well-structured and parseable
- [ ] Exit codes are correct for each error type
- [ ] Dry-run mode works correctly
- [ ] Explain mode shows plan without executing","notes":"### Testing & Logging
- Unit: JSON schema for --robot output; errors include machine-readable codes.
- Integration: CLI --robot export against fixtures verifies output path + logs array.
- E2E: script validates jq parsing and log bundle (command, args, exit code).
- Logging: ensure export_id correlates logs across stages.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T20:30:31.220926664Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T00:13:17.090670154Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T00:13:17.090583862Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["html-export"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-24uo","depends_on_id":"bd-2r58","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:38Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-24uo","depends_on_id":"bd-8w6y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:38Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-24uo","depends_on_id":"bd-u4me","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:38Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-3rd0b","title":"Add background color to search input area for visual separation","description":"## What

Add a subtle inset background to the search query input row, making it visually distinguishable from the surrounding search bar block. Currently the input area inherits the parent pane background, making the text field invisible as a distinct UI element.

## Where

- **app.rs:~11738-11747** — Query text rendering:
  ```rust
  let query_row = rows[0];
  let query_style = if self.input_mode == InputMode::Query && self.query.is_empty() {
      text_muted_style
  } else {
      styles.style(style_system::STYLE_TEXT_PRIMARY)
  };
  Paragraph::new(elide_text(&query_text, query_row.width as usize))
      .style(query_style)
      .render(query_row, frame);
  ```

## Target
Add a block with inset background BEFORE the query text:
```rust
// Inset background for text field affordance
let input_bg = if apply_style {
    Style::new().bg(to_packed(blend(resolved.surface, resolved.background, 0.5)))
} else { plain };
Block::new().style(input_bg).render(query_row, frame);
// Then render query text on top
Paragraph::new(elide_text(&query_text, ...))
    .style(query_style)
    .render(query_row, frame);
```

## Visual Effect
The input row becomes slightly darker/lighter than the surrounding block, creating a "text field" look similar to web input elements. The effect is SUBTLE — a 50% blend between surface and background, not a jarring color change.

## New Style Token (Optional)
Consider defining `STYLE_INPUT_BG` in style_system.rs:
```rust
pub const STYLE_INPUT_BG: &str = "input.bg";
sheet.define(STYLE_INPUT_BG, Style::new()
    .bg(to_packed(blend(resolved.surface, resolved.background, 0.5))));
```
This would make the input field background theme-aware and customizable.

## Required Tests

### Unit tests:
1. `test_search_input_has_background` — Render the search bar, verify the query row has a non-default background style applied (bg is Some).
2. `test_search_input_bg_differs_from_pane` — Verify the input bg color differs from STYLE_PANE_BASE bg.
3. `test_search_input_placeholder_visible` — With empty query, verify placeholder text ("<type to search>") is visible against the input background (contrast check).
4. `test_search_input_degradation` — At NoStyling, verify input bg drops to plain.

### Snapshot test:
5. Update `cassapp_results_wide.snap` to show the input area with visible background distinction.

## Considerations
- The blend ratio (0.5) may need tuning. Too strong → jarring; too subtle → invisible. Start with 0.5, test across all 6 presets, adjust if needed.
- On Light theme, the inset should be DARKER. On Dark theme, it should be LIGHTER (or slightly different shade of dark). The blend function handles this naturally since it interpolates between two theme colors.
- The pill row (row 2) and breadcrumb row (row 3) do NOT get the inset — only the query input row.
- This is a lower-impact visual change but contributes to the overall "designed" feel.

## Acceptance
- Query input row has visible background different from surrounding pane
- Placeholder text readable against background
- All 6 presets produce appropriate inset effect
- Degradation strips background at NoStyling+
- 4 unit tests + snapshot update pass
- No clippy warnings","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:33:54.169475277Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T19:57:02.079558495Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T19:57:02.079535091Z","close_reason":"Superseded by canonical FTUI bead graph under coding_agent_session_search-2dccg (Tracks A-K) with preserved scope and stronger dependency/test/logging coverage.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3rd0b","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:36:59.212531744Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-3s2b","title":"Test Coverage Hardening Epic (No-Mock)","description":"## Epic: Test Coverage Hardening (No-Mock Policy Compliant)

This epic addresses test coverage gaps while strictly adhering to the no-mock policy. Focus is on:
1. **Unit tests for pure functions** - Parsing, validation, transformation logic
2. **Edge case coverage** - Error paths, boundary conditions, malformed inputs
3. **Fixture expansion** - Real data for untested scenarios
4. **Assertion quality** - Descriptive messages for test failures

### Current State
- Overall unit test coverage: <5% (intentional)
- Integration test coverage: ~85%
- E2E coverage: ~100% of critical paths
- Mock exceptions: 6 (in allowlist)

### Philosophy (from TESTING.md)
> Mocks hide bugs; real code paths matter more.

### Coverage Gaps Identified
| Module | Unit Tests | Integration | Gap Type |
|--------|-----------|-------------|----------|
| Connectors (10+) | 0% | 100% | Edge case parsing |
| Pages/Wizard | ~20% | 80% | Path validation |
| FastEmbed | 0% | Integration | Model loading states |
| HTML Export | 0% | 95% | Encoding edge cases |
| Search Pipeline | <5% | 100% | Query parsing edge cases |

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All connector parsers have edge case unit tests (malformed JSONL, truncated files)
- [ ] Pages path validation has unit tests (traversal, encoding)
- [ ] Query parser has comprehensive unit tests (special chars, unicode)
- [ ] Assertion messages include context for all test failures
- [ ] Coverage stays >=60% (current threshold)
- [ ] No new mocks introduced (or added to allowlist with justification)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:21:10.882590430Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:24:57.558224565Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T22:24:57.558145719Z","close_reason":"All children completed: 819v (Pages Path Security), 6xnm (E2E Scripts), 27y8 (Connector Edge Cases), 1x2e (Assertion Quality), 335y (Query Parser Tests) - test coverage hardening complete","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-3t2r","title":"Task: Unit and integration tests for semantic search","description":"Comprehensive tests for semantic search in cass, including daemon client integration, embedding generation, reranking, and fallback handling.

## Test Categories

### 1. Daemon Client Tests
```rust
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_daemon_health_check() {
    let client = DaemonClient::new();
    match client.health().await {
        Ok(info) => {
            assert!(info.uptime_secs >= 0);
            tracing::info!(uptime = info.uptime_secs, "Daemon healthy");
        }
        Err(e) => tracing::warn!(error = %e, "Daemon not running"),
    }
}

#[tokio::test]
async fn test_embed_via_daemon() {
    let client = spawn_test_daemon().await;
    let result = client.embed(&["test query"], Some("all-MiniLM-L6-v2"), None).await;
    
    assert!(result.is_ok());
    let embeddings = result.unwrap();
    assert_eq!(embeddings.len(), 1);
    assert_eq!(embeddings[0].len(), 384);
}

#[tokio::test]
async fn test_rerank_via_daemon() {
    let client = spawn_test_daemon().await;
    let docs = vec!["relevant doc about auth", "unrelated doc", "another auth doc"];
    let scores = client.rerank("authentication", &docs, Some("flashrank-nano")).await.unwrap();
    
    assert_eq!(scores.len(), 3);
    assert!(scores[0] > scores[1], "Relevant doc should score higher");
}
```

### 2. Semantic Search Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_semantic_search_returns_relevant_sessions() {
    let index = build_test_index_with_ground_truth();
    let queries = load_test_queries();
    
    for query in queries {
        let results = index.search(&query.text, SearchOpts::semantic()).unwrap();
        let relevant_found = results.iter().take(10)
            .filter(|r| query.relevant_ids.contains(&r.session_id))
            .count();
        
        tracing::info!(query = %query.text, found = relevant_found, "Search quality");
        assert!(relevant_found > 0);
    }
}

#[test]
fn test_hybrid_search_combines_bm25_and_semantic() {
    let index = build_test_index();
    let query = "authentication middleware";
    
    let bm25 = index.search(query, SearchOpts::bm25()).unwrap();
    let semantic = index.search(query, SearchOpts::semantic()).unwrap();
    let hybrid = index.search(query, SearchOpts::hybrid()).unwrap();
    
    // Hybrid should have elements from both
    let bm25_ids: HashSet<_> = bm25.iter().map(|r| &r.id).collect();
    let semantic_ids: HashSet<_> = semantic.iter().map(|r| &r.id).collect();
    let hybrid_ids: HashSet<_> = hybrid.iter().map(|r| &r.id).collect();
    
    let overlap = hybrid_ids.intersection(&bm25_ids).count() + 
                  hybrid_ids.intersection(&semantic_ids).count();
    assert!(overlap > 0, "Hybrid should blend BM25 and semantic results");
}
```

### 3. Fallback Tests
```rust
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_search_works_without_daemon() {
    let engine = CassSearchEngine::new_direct(); // No daemon
    let results = engine.search("test query", SearchOpts::semantic()).await;
    assert!(results.is_ok());
}

#[tokio::test]
async fn test_graceful_fallback_on_daemon_crash() {
    let engine = CassSearchEngine::new_with_daemon();
    // First search warms daemon
    let _ = engine.search("warmup", SearchOpts::semantic()).await;
    
    // Kill daemon
    stop_daemon().await;
    
    // Search should still work via direct inference
    let results = engine.search("after crash", SearchOpts::semantic()).await;
    assert!(results.is_ok());
}
```

### 4. Reranking Tests
```rust
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_reranking_improves_relevance() {
    let index = build_test_index_with_ground_truth();
    let queries = load_test_queries();
    
    let mut without_rerank_mrr = 0.0;
    let mut with_rerank_mrr = 0.0;
    
    for query in &queries {
        without_rerank_mrr += compute_mrr(&index, &query.text, false, &query.relevant);
        with_rerank_mrr += compute_mrr(&index, &query.text, true, &query.relevant);
    }
    
    let n = queries.len() as f64;
    tracing::info!(
        without = without_rerank_mrr / n,
        with = with_rerank_mrr / n,
        "Reranking impact"
    );
    
    assert!(with_rerank_mrr >= without_rerank_mrr * 0.95);
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-3tc1k","title":"Restore richer ratatui-era visual hierarchy in FrankenTUI results/detail surfaces","description":"Follow-up parity pass beyond initial mitigations: compare old ratatui and current ftui surfaces, recover high-signal visual hierarchy (agent color differentiation, snippet/readability density, accent contrast), and lock with snapshot evidence.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T04:51:39.180419032Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T09:59:21.645837289Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T09:59:10.783961744Z","close_reason":"Completed follow-up agent-tinted hierarchy pass with regression tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankentui","parity","ui"],"comments":[{"id":489,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3tc1k","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed parity pass for results/detail visual hierarchy.

What changed:
- Normalized result-score visuals to a unified 0..10 scale (for both fractional 0..1 relevance and legacy 0..10 scores) so bar magnitude and high/mid/low tiers are meaningful again.
- Added explicit match-type cue () in non-compact result metadata for faster scanability.
- Increased compact snippet readability by using muted text weight for inline preview.
- Shifted detail metadata score display to include normalized context (e.g. ).
- Replaced emoji-heavy agent icons with deterministic single-glyph symbols to avoid fallback artifacts and width jitter in terminals.

Regression coverage added:
- score_style_routes_to_correct_tier now verifies fractional-score tier mapping.
- score_normalization_and_bar_labels_support_fractional_relevance
- results_metadata_includes_match_type_cue_for_scanning
- agent_icons_are_single_char_glyphs

- cargo check --all-targets: PASS
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings: PASS

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-3tl5t","title":"Unpin install scripts from v0.1.64 and update version to current","description":"## What

The install scripts (install.sh and install.ps1) and README are pinned to v0.1.64, the last ratatui build. The README explicitly states: "Release gate note: install scripts are currently pinned to v0.1.64 (last ratatui build) unless you explicitly pass --version/-Version." Once the visual polish work is complete, this pin must be removed and the version bumped to the current ftui-based release.

## Where

- **install.sh** — Contains version pin (likely a `VERSION=` variable or hardcoded in download URL)
- **install.ps1** — Same pin for Windows PowerShell installer
- **README.md:17** — References `--version v0.1.64` in install command
- **README.md:25** — Release gate note explaining the pin

## Current State
```bash
# README.md line 17
curl -fsSL "..." | bash -s -- --easy-mode --verify --version v0.1.64
```
```
# README.md line 25
Release gate note: install scripts are currently pinned to v0.1.64 (last ratatui build)
```

## Target
1. Determine the current version of the codebase (check Cargo.toml `version` field)
2. Update install.sh to use the new version (or remove the pin entirely if install scripts auto-detect latest)
3. Update install.ps1 similarly
4. Update README.md install commands to reference the new version
5. Remove or update the "Release gate note" — it should either be removed entirely or changed to note the new version

## Considerations
- This task should ONLY be done after ALL visual polish tasks are verified as complete. It is the final gate.
- The version in Cargo.toml may need to be bumped (e.g., from 0.1.x to 0.2.0 to mark the ftui milestone).
- Verify that the install scripts actually work with the new version before updating the README. Test on a clean system or in CI.
- The README install commands may also need the GitHub release tag to exist. Coordinate with the release process.
- Consider whether to create a GitHub release (tag + binary artifacts) as part of this task or leave that as a separate step.

## Acceptance
- install.sh no longer pinned to v0.1.64
- install.ps1 no longer pinned to v0.1.64
- README.md install commands reference current version
- Release gate note removed or updated
- Install scripts tested and working with new version
- This is the LAST task closed in the epic","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:33:32.643014744Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T19:57:02.107453594Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T19:57:02.107435039Z","close_reason":"Superseded by canonical FTUI bead graph under coding_agent_session_search-2dccg (Tracks A-K) with preserved scope and stronger dependency/test/logging coverage.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3tl5t","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:37:00.238746007Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3tl5t","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2vb81","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:35:21.933345173Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
